title: Feature request: podman network rm: add -a, --all option
body:
to remove all networks.

Similar to the flags on `podman rm` and `podman rmi` to remove all containers and images.

comments.body:
Interested in opening a PR?  I think this might be a little more difficult, and potentially dangerous.
@mheon @Luap99 PTAL
It should be fine but we have to ensure that we do not delete the default podman network. We already support network prune.
I didn't know about `network prune`. That also works for me. You can close this ticket if you want.
Perhaps we could add information to podman network rm  about this capability?  Would that have helped you?
It's worth mentioning. Also, `prune` could have been a flag on `podman network rm`.
Sure, but we are trying to match the Docker CLI, so they have a docker network prune command and so do we.
